author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm III

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A psalm of David, when he fled from the face of Abessalon his son.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The words, “I slept, and took rest; and rose, for the Lord will take me up,” lead us to believe that this Psalm is to be understood as in the Person of Christ; for they sound more applicable to the Passion and Resurrection of our Lord, than to that history in which David’s flight is described from the face of his rebellious son.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And, since it is written of Christ’s disciples, “The sons of the bridegroom fast not as long as the bridegroom is with them;” it is no wonder if by his undutiful son be here meant that undutiful disciple who betrayed Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From whose face although it may be understood historically that He fled, when on his departure He withdrew with the rest to the mountain; yet in a spiritual sense, when the Son of God, that is the Power and Wisdom of God, abandoned the mind of Judas; when the Devil wholly occupied him; as it is written, “The Devil entered into his heart,” may it be well understood that Christ fled from his face; not that Christ gave place to the Devil, but that on Christ’s departure the Devil took possession.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Which departure, I suppose, is called a flight in this Psalm, because of its quickness; which is indicated also by the word of our Lord, saying, “That thou doest, do quickly.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So even in common conversation we say of anything that does not come to mind, it has fled from me; and of a man of much learning we say, nothing flies from him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore truth fled from the mind of Judas, when it ceased to enlighten him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But Absalom, as some interpret, in the Latin tongue signifies, Patris pax, a father’s peace.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And it may seem strange, whether in the history of the kings, when Absalom carried on war against his father; or in the history of the New Testament, when Judas was the betrayer of our Lord; how “father’s peace” can be understood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But both in the former place they who read carefully, see that David in that war was at peace with his son, who even with sore grief lamented his death, saying, “O Absalom, my son, would God I had died for thee!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And in the history of the New Testament by that so great and so wonderful forbearance of our Lord; in that He bore so long with him as if good, when He was not ignorant of his thoughts; in that He admitted him to the Supper in which He committed and delivered to His disciples the figure of His Body and Blood; finally, in that He received the kiss of peace at the very time of His betrayal; it is easily understood how Christ showed peace to His betrayer, although he was laid waste by the intestine war of so abominable a device.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And therefore is Absalom called “father’s peace,” because his father had the peace, which he had not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O Lord, how are they multiplied that trouble me!” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So multiplied indeed were they, that one even from the number of His disciples was not wanting, who was added to the number of His persecutors.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Many rise up against me; many say unto my soul, There is no salvation for him in his God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is clear that if they had had any idea that He would rise again, assuredly they would not have slain Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To this end are those speeches, “Let Him come down from the cross, if He be the Son of God;” and again, “He saved others, Himself He cannot save.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, neither would Judas have betrayed Him, if he had not been of the number of those who despised Christ, saying, “There is no salvation for Him in His God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But Thou, O Lord, art my taker.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is said to God in the nature of man, for the taking of man is, the Word made Flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even He calls God his glory, whom the Word of God so took, that God became one with Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the proud learn, who unwillingly hear, when it is said to them, “For what hast thou that thou didst not receive?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now if thou didst receive it, why dost thou glory as if thou hadst not received it?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the lifter up of my head” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I think that this should be here taken of the human mind, which is not unreasonably called the head of the soul; which so inhered in, and in a sort coalesced with, the supereminent excellency of the Word taking man, that it was not laid aside by so great humiliation of the Passion.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “With my voice have I cried unto the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 4); that is, not with the voice of the body, which is drawn out with the sound of the reverberation of the air; but with the voice of the heart, which to men speaks not, but with God sounds as a cry.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By this voice Susanna was heard; and with this voice the Lord Himself commanded that prayer should be made in closets, that is, in the recesses of the heart noiselessly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nor would one easily say that prayer is not made with this voice, if no sound of words is uttered from the body; since even when in silence we pray within the heart, if thoughts interpose alien from the mind of one praying, it cannot yet be said, “With my voice have I cried unto the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nor is this rightly said, save when the soul alone, taking to itself nothing of the flesh, and nothing of the aims of the flesh, in prayer, speaks to God, where He only hears.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But even this is called a cry by reason of the strength of its intention.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And He heard me out of His holy mountain.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We have the Lord Himself called a mountain by the Prophet, as it is written, “The stone that was cut out without hands grew to the size of a mountain.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But this cannot be taken of His Person, unless peradventure He would speak thus, out of myself, as of His holy mountain He heard me, when He dwelt in me, that is, in this very mountain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But it is more plain and unembarrassed, if we understand that God out of His justice heard.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it was just that He should raise again from the dead the Innocent who was slain, and to whom evil had been recompensed for good, and that He should render to the persecutor a meet reward, who repaid Him evil for good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we read, “Thy justice is as the mountains of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I slept, and took rest” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It may be not unsuitably remarked, that it is expressly said, “I,” to signify that of His own Will He underwent death, according to that, “Therefore doth My Father love Me, because I lay down My life, that I might take it again.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No man taketh it from Me; I have power to lay it down, and I have power to take it again.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, saith He, you have not taken Me as though against My will, and slain Me; but “I slept, and took rest; and rose, for the Lord will take me up.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Scripture contains numberless instances of sleep being put for death; as the Apostle says, “I would not have you to be ignorant, brethren, concerning them which are asleep.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nor need we make any question why it is added, “took rest,” seeing that it has already been said, “I slept.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Repetitions of this kind are usual in Scripture, as we have pointed out many in the second Psalm.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But some copies have, “I slept, and was cast into a deep sleep.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And different copies express it differently, according to the possible renderings of the Greek words, ἐγὼ δš ἐκοιμήθην καὶ ὕπνωσα.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Unless perhaps sleeping may be taken of one dying, but sleep of one dead: so that sleeping may be the transition into sleep, as awakening is the transition into wakefulness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us not deem these repetitions in the sacred writings empty ornaments of speech.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I slept, and took rest,” is therefore well understood as “I gave Myself up to My Passion, and death ensued.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I rose, for the Lord will take Me up.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the more to be remarked, how that in one sentence the Psalmist has used a verb of past and future time.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he has said, both “I rose,” which is the past, and “will take Me up,” which is the future; seeing that assuredly the rising again could not be without that taking up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But in prophecy the future is well joined to the past, whereby both are signified.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since things which are prophesied of as yet to come in reference to time are future; but in reference to the knowledge of those who prophesy they are already to be viewed as done.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Verbs of the present tense are also mixed in, which shall be treated of in their proper place when they occur.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will not fear the thousands of people that surround me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is written in the Gospels how great a multitude stood around Him as He was suffering, and on the cross.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Arise, O Lord, save me, O my God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is not said to God, “Arise,” as if asleep or lying down, but it is usual in holy Scripture to attribute to God what He doeth in us; not indeed universally, but where it can be done suitably; as when He is said to speak, when by His gift Prophets speak, and Apostles, or whatsoever messengers of the truth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hence that text, “Would you have proof of Christ, who speaketh in me?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he doth not say, of Christ, by whose enlightening or order I speak; but he attributes at once the speaking itself to Him, by whose gift he spake.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Since Thou hast smitten all who oppose me without a cause.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is not to be pointed as if it were one sentence, “Arise, O Lord, save me, O my God; since Thou hast smitten all who oppose me without a cause.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He did not therefore save Him, because He smote His enemies; but rather He being saved, He smote them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore it belongs to what follows, so that the sense is this; “Since Thou hast smitten all who oppose me without a cause, Thou hast broken the teeth of the sinners;” that is, thereby hast Thou broken the teeth of the sinners, since Thou hast smitten all who oppose me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is forsooth the punishment of the opposers, whereby their teeth have been broken, that is, the words of sinners rending with their cursing the Son of God, brought to nought, as it were to dust; so that we may understand “teeth” thus, as words of cursing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of which teeth the Apostle speaks, “If ye bite one another, take heed that ye be not consumed one of another.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The teeth of sinners can also be taken as the chiefs of sinners; by whose authority each one is cut off from the fellowship of godly livers, and as it were incorporated with evil livers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To these teeth are opposed the Church’s teeth, by whose authority believers are cut off from the error of the Gentiles and divers opinions, and are translated into that fellowship which is the body of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With these teeth Peter was told to eat the animals when they had been killed, that is, by killing in the Gentiles what they were, and changing them into what he was himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of these teeth too of the Church it is said, “Thy teeth are as a flock of shorn sheep, coming up from the bath, whereof every one beareth twins, and there is not one barren among them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These are they who prescribe rightly, and as they prescribe, live; who do what is written, “Let your works shine before men, that they may bless your Father which is in heaven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For moved by their authority, they believe God who speaketh and worketh through these men; and separated from the world, to which they were once conformed, they pass over into the members of the Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And rightly therefore are they, through whom such things are done, called teeth like to shorn sheep; for they have laid aside the burdens of earthly cares, and coming up from the bath, from the washing away of the filth of the world by the Sacrament of Baptism, every one beareth twins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they fulfil the two commandments, of which it is said, “On these two commandments hang all the Law and the Prophets;” loving God with all their heart, and with all their soul, and with all their mind, and their neighbour as themselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “There is not one barren among them,” for much fruit they render unto God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: According to this sense then it is to be thus understood, “Thou hast broken the teeth of the sinners,” that is, Thou hast brought the chiefs of the sinners to nought, by smiting all who oppose Me without a cause.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the chiefs according to the Gospel history persecuted Him, whilst the lower people honoured Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Salvation is of the Lord; and upon Thy people be Thy blessing” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In one sentence the Psalmist has enjoined men what to believe, and has prayed for believers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when it is said, “Salvation is of the Lord,” the words are addressed to men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nor does it follow, “And upon Thy people” be “Thy blessing,” in such wise as that the whole is spoken to men, but there is a change into prayer addressed to God Himself, for the very people to whom it was said, “Salvation is of the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What else then doth he say but this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let no man presume on himself, seeing that it is of the Lord to save from the death of sin; for, “Wretched man that I am, who shall deliver me from the body of this death?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The grace of God through Jesus Christ our Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But do Thou, O Lord, bless Thy people, who look for salvation from Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This Psalm can be taken as in the Person of Christ another way; which is that whole Christ should speak.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I mean by whole, with His body, of which He is the Head, according to the Apostle, who says, “Ye are the body of Christ, and the members.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He therefore is the Head of this body; wherefore in another place he saith, “But doing the truth in love, we may increase in Him in all things, who is the Head, Christ, from whom the whole body is joined together and compacted.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the Prophet then at once, the Church, and her Head (the Church founded amidst the storms of persecution throughout the whole world, which we know already to have come to pass), speaks, “O Lord, how are they multiplied that trouble me! many rise up against me;” wishing to exterminate the Christian name.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Many say unto my soul, There is no salvation for him in his God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they would not otherwise hope that they could destroy the Church, branching out so very far and wide, unless they believed that God had no care thereof.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But Thou, O Lord, art my taker;” in Christ of course.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For into that flesh the Church too hath been taken by the Word, “who was made flesh, and dwelt in us;” for that “In heavenly places hath He made us to sit together with Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When the Head goes before, the other members will follow; for, “Who shall separate us from the love of Christ?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Justly then does the Church say, “Thou art my taker.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: My glory;” for she doth not attribute her excellency to herself, seeing that she knoweth by whose grace and mercy she is what she is.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the lifter up of my head,” of Him, namely, who, “the First-born from the dead,” ascended up into heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “With my voice have I cried unto the Lord, and He heard me out of His holy mountain.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the prayer of all the Saints, the odour of sweetness, which ascends up in the sight of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For now the Church is heard out of this mountain, which is also her head; or, out of that justice of God, by which both His elect are set free, and their persecutors punished.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the people of God also say, “I slept, and took rest; and rose, for the Lord will take me up;” that they may be joined, and cleave to their Head.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to this people is it said, “Awake thou that sleepest, and arise from the dead, and Christ shall lay hold on thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since they are taken out of sinners, of whom it is said generally, “But they that sleep, sleep in the night.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them say moreover, “I will not fear the thousands of people that surround me;” of the heathen verily that compass me about to extinguish everywhere, if they could, the Christian name.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But how should they be feared, when by the blood of the martyrs in Christ, as by oil, the ardour of love is inflamed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Arise, O Lord, save me, O my God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The body can address this to its own Head.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For at His rising the body was saved; who “ascended up on high, led captivity captive, gave gifts unto men.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this is said by the Prophet, in the secret purpose of God, until that ripe harvest which is spoken of in the Gospel, whose salvation is in His Resurrection, who vouchsafed to die for us, shed out our Lord to the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Since Thou hast smitten all who oppose me without a cause, Thou hast broken the teeth of the sinners.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now while the Church hath rule, the enemies of the Christian name are smitten with confusion; and, whether their curses or their chiefs, brought to nought.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Believe then, O man, that “salvation is of the Lord: and,” Thou, O Lord, may “Thy blessing” be “upon Thy people.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Each one too of us may say, when a multitude of vices and lusts leads the resisting mind in the law of sin, “O Lord, how are they multiplied that trouble me! many rise up against me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And, since despair of recovery generally creeps in through the accumulation of vices, as though these same vices were mocking the soul, or even as though the Devil and his angels through their poisonous suggestions were at work to make us despair, it is said with great truth, “Many say unto my soul, There is no salvation for him in his God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But Thou, O Lord, art my taker.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this is our hope, that He hath vouchsafed to take the nature of man in Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My glory;” according to that rule, that no one should ascribe ought to himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the lifter up of my head;” either of Him, who is the Head of us all, or of the spirit of each several one of us, which is the head of the soul and body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “the head of the woman is the man, and the head of the man is Christ.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the mind is lifted up, when it can be said already, “With the mind I serve the law of God;” that the rest of man may be reduced to peaceable submission, when in the resurrection of the flesh “death is swallowed up in victory.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “With my voice I have cried unto the Lord;” with that most inward and intensive voice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And He heard me out of His holy mountain;” Him, through whom He hath succoured us, through whose mediation He heareth us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I slept, and took rest; and rose, for the Lord will take me up.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who of the faithful is not able to say this, when he calls to mind the death of his sins, and the gift of regeneration?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will not fear the thousands of people that surround me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Besides those which the Church universally hath borne and beareth, each one also hath temptations, by which, when compassed about, he may speak these words, “Arise, O Lord; save me, O my God:” that is, make me to arise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Since Thou hast smitten all who oppose me without a cause:” it is well in God’s determinate purpose said of the Devil and his angels; who rage not only against the whole body of Christ, but also against each one in particular.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast broken the teeth of the sinners.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Each man hath those that revile him, he hath too the prime authors of vice, who strive to cut him off from the body of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But “salvation is of the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Pride is to be guarded against, and we must say, “My soul cleaved after Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And upon Thy people” be “Thy blessing:” that is, upon each one of us.
